apis.name: preferences
apis.purpose: Module import for user preferences KV storage

apis.name: preferences.getPreferences
apis.purpose: Get Preferences instance (callback/Promise, name or Options)

apis.name: preferences.getPreferencesSync
apis.purpose: Synchronously get Preferences instance (API 10+)

apis.name: preferences.deletePreferences
apis.purpose: Delete Preferences instance and its persistence file

apis.name: preferences.removePreferencesFromCache
apis.purpose: Remove Preferences instance from cache only

apis.name: preferences.removePreferencesFromCacheSync
apis.purpose: Synchronously remove Preferences from cache (API 10+)

apis.name: preferences.isStorageTypeSupported
apis.purpose: Check if a storage mode is supported on current platform (API 18+)

apis.name: preferences.StorageType
apis.purpose: Enum for storage mode (XML=0, GSKV=1) (API 18+)

apis.name: preferences.Options
apis.purpose: Configuration options for Preferences instance (API 10+)

apis.name: preferences.MAX_KEY_LENGTH
apis.purpose: Maximum key length constant (1024 bytes)

apis.name: preferences.MAX_VALUE_LENGTH
apis.purpose: Maximum value length constant (16 MB)

apis.name: preferences.Preferences
apis.purpose: Preferences instance interface providing get/put/delete/has/clear/flush/on/off methods

apis.name: Preferences.get
apis.purpose: Get value by key from cached Preferences instance

apis.name: Preferences.getSync
apis.purpose: Synchronously get value by key (API 10+)

apis.name: Preferences.getAll
apis.purpose: Get all key-value pairs from cached Preferences instance

apis.name: Preferences.getAllSync
apis.purpose: Synchronously get all key-value pairs (API 10+)

apis.name: Preferences.put
apis.purpose: Write data into cached Preferences instance

apis.name: Preferences.putSync
apis.purpose: Synchronously write data into cached Preferences (API 10+)

apis.name: Preferences.has
apis.purpose: Check if a key exists in cached Preferences instance

apis.name: Preferences.hasSync
apis.purpose: Synchronously check if a key exists (API 10+)

apis.name: Preferences.delete
apis.purpose: Delete a key-value pair from cached Preferences instance

apis.name: Preferences.deleteSync
apis.purpose: Synchronously delete a key-value pair (API 10+)

apis.name: Preferences.flush
apis.purpose: Persist cached Preferences data to file (XML mode only)

apis.name: Preferences.flushSync
apis.purpose: Synchronously persist cached data to file (API 14+)

apis.name: Preferences.clear
apis.purpose: Clear all data from cached Preferences instance

apis.name: Preferences.clearSync
apis.purpose: Synchronously clear all data (API 10+)

apis.name: Preferences.on('change')
apis.purpose: Subscribe to all data change events

apis.name: Preferences.on('multiProcessChange')
apis.purpose: Subscribe to multi-process data change events (API 10+)

apis.name: Preferences.on('dataChange')
apis.purpose: Subscribe to precise key-level data change events (API 12+)

apis.name: Preferences.off('change')
apis.purpose: Unsubscribe from data change events

apis.name: Preferences.off('multiProcessChange')
apis.purpose: Unsubscribe from multi-process data change events (API 10+)

apis.name: Preferences.off('dataChange')
apis.purpose: Unsubscribe from precise key-level data change events (API 12+)

apis.name: preferences.ValueType
apis.purpose: Supported value types for Preferences storage

notableFindings: Import via `import { preferences } from '@kit.ArkData'` (not from @kit.ArkData or @ohos.data.preferences directly)
Preferences does NOT guarantee process concurrency safety — risk of file corruption in multi-process scenarios
API 18+ introduced StorageType enum: XML (default, requires manual flush) and GSKV (real-time persistence, no flush needed)
Key max 1024 bytes, Value max 16 MB
on('dataChange') from API 12+ supports precise key-level subscription with new value in callback
flush() only needed in XML mode; GSKV mode persists data in real-time automatically
dataGroupId in Options enables cross-process data sharing (Stage model only, requires marketplace approval)
missingApis: dataPreferences (not an API — this is a common variable name for a Preferences instance; the proper API is `preferences`)
